category_title: Third-grade boys with E/BD displaying disruptive behaviors during reading instruction
student_description: This student exhibits externalizing and disruptive behaviors (talking out, noises, work refusal) during academic tasks, particularly reading and group work, and does not respond to general schoolwide behavioral expectations. They uniquely require highly structured, frequent feedback through a Check-In, Check-Out (CICO) system, including daily goal-setting, incremental progress tracking, and home-school communication.

criteria.rationale: This student requires a predictable, daily routine of setting and reviewing behavioral goals with a mentor to increase their frequency of meeting expectations.
criteria.check: The long-term plan must establish a daily cycle that includes a morning check-in to set a behavioral goal and an afternoon check-out to review progress and provide reinforcement.

criteria.rationale: Extending the feedback loop to the student's home environment provides the consistent, multi-tiered reinforcement this student needs to manage disruptive behaviors.
criteria.check: The long-term plan must include a requirement for the student to take their daily progress report home to be signed by a parent/guardian and returned the next day.
